"He is no respecter of persons; but in every nation he that fears God and works righteousness is accepted of him." (Lectures on Faith 3:17.) These words are but a restatement of what Peter said in the house of Cornelius. (Acts 10:34-35.) And Paul said simply: "There is no respect of persons with God." (Romans 2:11.) (Bruce R. Mc Conkie, A New Witness for the Articles of Faith, p.173)

This verse should be taken in the sense of Romans 2:28-29, "he is not a real Jew who is one outwardly [but] he is a Jew who is one inwardly."
